extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent), i.e. no reachable state has opted_out=true AND sent=true. The transition t_resume_glitch fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and ticks>=10) and sets sent:=true, directly producing the forbidden state.

Witness execution (12 events, within the 14-event bound), starting from initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0):
1. optout (t_optout, guard true): opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2-11. tick x10 (t_tick, guard true): ticks increments 0->1->...->10 (capped at 10), sent stays false. After the 10th tick: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=10.
12. send_attempt: now opted_out=true and ticks>=10, so t_resume_glitch is enabled and sets sent:=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true, sent=true.

This state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating opted_out implies not sent. (Although t_send_blocked is also enabled on this send_attempt, t_resume_glitch is a listed, enabled transition with a strictly more specific guard, so the execution that takes it is reachable; the existence of one violating execution suffices.) Property is violated.
